entries.testName: Test Case API > creates a test case with the correct priority
entries.aiExplanation: The test expected the created test case to have priority 'P2', but the API response returned priority 'P1'. This mismatch likely stems from either the test sending an incorrect 'priority' value of 'P1' in the POST request body (instead of 'P2'), or the mock/stub server ('/posts' endpoint, which resembles a placeholder API like JSONPlaceholder) being configured with a hardcoded/incorrect default value of 'P1' that doesn't reflect the actual request payload.
entries.aiSuggestedFix: Check the request payload being sent in the POST /posts call in api.test.ts around line 23 — ensure the 'priority' field is explicitly set to 'P2' in the request body. If using a mock server or fixture, verify that the mock response for this endpoint returns 'P2' instead of the hardcoded 'P1'. Also confirm the assertion is comparing against the correct expected value.

entries.testName: Login Page > shows the dashboard heading after login
entries.errorMessage: expected 'Example Domain' to be 'Welcome to your Dashboard' // Object.is equality
entries.aiExplanation: After the login action, the test asserts that the dashboard heading text equals 'Welcome to your Dashboard', but the page state shows the browser is still/again on 'https://example.com/' with title 'Example Domain'. This means the login submission did not navigate to the actual application's dashboard page — likely the login form action/URL is misconfigured, the app under test's base URL is not set correctly (pointing to example.com instead of the real app), or the login request failed/redirected to a default page, so the dashboard heading assertion is comparing against the wrong page's content entirely.
entries.aiSuggestedFix: Verify the test's base URL/config (e.g., baseURL in playwright.config.ts or environment variable) points to the correct application under test rather than example.com. Also confirm the login flow actually submits credentials and waits for navigation to the dashboard (e.g., await page.waitForURL('**/dashboard') or await expect(page).toHaveURL(/dashboard/) before asserting the heading). Add explicit waits for the post-login redirect and check that login credentials/selectors used in the test are correct so the login succeeds instead of leaving the browser on the placeholder example.com page.
